name: Phoenix-Tailed Shrimp
recipeInstructions:
1. Carefully remove the shells from the shrimp, leaving the tail sections intact.
2. Devein and wash the shrimp under cold running water; pat dry with paper towels. In a dish or bowl, marinate briefly in a mixture of vodka, salt, and pepper while you make the batter.
3. In a mixing bowl, combine flour and baking powder; gradually add cold water, whisking until smooth.
4. Heat 2 cups oil in a wok until it reaches 350°F (175°C). Add 1 tablespoon hot oil to the batter and stir to combine.
5. Dip each shrimp by its tail into the batter (do not dip the tail), then slide it into the hot oil. Deep-fry all the shrimp, a few at a time, for about 2 minutes per batch until golden brown.
6. Drain on paper towels, and serve tails up in a serving dish with the salt/pepper mixture for dipping.
Makes 8 to 10 appetizer servings.
FOR DIPPING: Combine coarse salt and peppercorns in a bowl. Brown in a dry frying pan over high heat, then grind.
